methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $103M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zeitview
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Oxa is a Series D autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $103M to date. The broader sector includes five private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Zeitview) and no direct public comparables, reflecting the early-stage maturity of commercial autonomous systems.

Valuation analysis applied one applicable method: Stage-Progression, which uses the $103M in total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D to later-stage transitions. This approach yields an implied valuation of $154M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data within the narrow peer set; while four same-sector peers exist, none met the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints after filtering for stage and valuation tier. Attempting a broader all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $131M (low) to $178M (high), centered on $154M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include Oxa's specific revenue traction, unit economics, and path to commercialization relative to peers—data not available in this review. The reliance on a single method also limits conviction; stronger triangulation would require either expanded comparables data or access to recent transaction or financing multiples in autonomous systems.
